Odia.skill: Read various types of materials in the Odia language (including newspapers, magazines, stories, and internet publications) to understand and write your preferences, comments, and expressions in the language or in braille

Science.skill: Preparing and explaining graphical data, for instance, plotting distance against time graphs.

Simple techniques will be introduced to extract answers from asked questions. For instance, how to identify substances derived from colored flowers and how to determine whether green leaves contain chlorophyll.

SST.skill: Explain the causes of forest fires, landslides, man-made disasters, and ways to mitigate disasters.
Explain the working of the Indian judicial system by giving examples of some important cases.

Odia.skill: Read other materials alongside your textbook (such as children literature, main news articles, magazines, posters, etc.) and gain an understanding of them.
Use correct grammar in framing sentences in Language use appropriate word to frame sentences
Explain the meanings of unfamiliar words from word lists
Reads textual/non-textual materials in Language/ Braille with comprehension.
Interprets quotations, sayings and proverb etc.
While reading any text, understand the author’s viewpoint and express its similarities and dissimilarities based on your own experiences.

EVS.skill: Describe the various skills involved in daily life (such as farming, hunting, weaving, and handicrafts) along with the importance of training (the role of programs) in enhancing these skills.

Identify specific characteristics, such as shape (head, limbs, teeth, skin surface), habitat (whether domesticated or wild), fruits, vegetables, and spices, along with their life cycles, uses (such as for food, medicine, decoration, etc.), and sensory attributes (such as smell, taste, preferences).

Present various methods of human livelihood and the necessary resources of food and water; the processes include the production of materials from agriculture, preparation of various dishes, preservation of different foods, exploration, and protection of water sources.
Discuss the interdependence among animals, plants, and humans (for example – livestock management, fodder cultivation, etc.).

Identify and differentiate between various items related to past and present (such as transportation, goods, homes, materials, tools, skills, agriculture, etc.) and provide insights regarding their origins.

English.skill: Infers the meaning of unfamiliar words by reading them in contexts.

Maths.skill: Determine the factors, prime factors, cube, and cube roots of the given numbers.
Using the divisibility rules for 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 9, and 11, determine the factors, prime factors, cube, and cube roots of the provided numbers.
Demonstrate mathematical operations used in daily life.
Solve problems using the measurement formulas of various shapes.
Use symbols to denote fractional parts like one-half, one-fourth, and three-fourths.
Recognize the structure of multiplication and division up to the 9-times table.

Science.skill: Materials and living things will be classified based on their properties, for example - metals and non-metals, kharif and rabi crops, beneficial and harmful microorganisms, sexual and asexual reproduction, cosmic objects and non-renewable natural resources.
Understanding concepts related to environmental science, such as countering acidity in soil, conducting soil tests, and finding ways to prevent soil erosion.
Efforts will be made towards environmental protection, such as maintaining cleanliness in general places, reducing pollution-causing substances, and tree plantation to prevent soil erosion.
This section deals with the application of chemical reactions. Examples include acid-base reactions, rusting, and identification methods.

This involves relating processes and events to their causes. Examples include the relationship between wind speed and air pressure, various soil types and crop production, and the impact of groundwater depletion on human activities.

Apply the learning of scientific concepts to daily life, eg., - purifying water, separating biodegradable and non-biodegradable materials, increasing crop productivity, using appropriate metals and alloys for different purposes, increasing/ decreasing friction, and overcoming misconceptions and misconceptions related to puberty.

SST.skill: Explain the election process of the Lok Sabha.
Discuss the factors affecting biodiversity in flora and fauna (such as climate change, soil erosion, etc.).
Compare the policies of different rulers.
Discuss the significance of equality in a democracy.
Discuss the livelihood and living conditions of populations residing in geographical areas like tribal communities and rural settlements.
Identify the various layers of the Earth's atmosphere, types of rocks, and the different layers of the atmosphere using diagrams.

miscon.subject: Odia
miscon.stem: ଜଙ୍ଗଲ କ୍ଷୟ ର ମୂଖ୍ୟ କାରଣ କଣ ହୋଇଛି?

opts.A: ନୂଆଁ କାରଖାନା ଓ ଶିଳ୍ପ ଅଞ୍ଚଳ ସ୍ଥାପନ।
opts.B: ଜଙ୍ଗଲକୁ ଘରବାଡ଼ିରେ ପରିଣତ କରି ଶହର ବିସ୍ତାର।
opts.C: ଚାଷଜମି ବଢ଼ାଇବା ପାଇଁ ଜଙ୍ଗଲ ଶୁନ୍ୟ କରିବା

miscon.text: Deforestation was blamed on factories and industry. The main cause is clearing forest to expand farmland.
miscon.skill: By studying a subject, comprehend the concepts that may exist within it.

miscon.subject: EVS
miscon.stem: On a cold night, Reena wrapped one plant in a plastic sheet and left another open. In the morning she saw water droplets only on the plastic sheet. Which process best explains this?
miscon.text: Children chose evaporation. The droplets form by condensation, water vapour turning to liquid on the cool sheet.
miscon.skill: Identify situations related to various events, including estimating conditions (distance, weight, time duration) and using regional units of measurement (like yards, feet, miles) while establishing relationships between various causes and effects through simple techniques (examples - condensation, evaporation, separation, etc.) related to the lifespan of flowers, fruits, and other natural resources.

miscon.subject: Science
miscon.stem: ନିମ୍ନରେ ଥିବା ଦୂରତା-ସମୟ ଗ୍ରାଫ୍ ଜଣେ ସାଇକେଲ ଚାଳକଙ୍କ ଗତିକୁ ଦର୍ଶାଏ । 4 ରୁ 6 ସେକେଣ୍ଡ ମଧ୍ୟରେ ସମତଳ ଭୂସମାନ୍ତର ରେଖା କ'ଣ ପ୍ରତିନିଧିତ୍ୱ କରେ?

opts.A: ସାଇକେଲ ଚାଳକ ସ୍ଥିର ବେଗରେ ଗତି କରୁଛନ୍ତି
opts.B: ସାଇକେଲ ଚାଳକ ଗତି କରୁନାହାଁନ୍ତି; ସେ ଅଟକି ଯାଇଛନ୍ତି
opts.C: ସାଇକେଲ ଚାଳକ ସେଠାରେ ନିଜର ଗତି ବଢ଼ାଉଛନ୍ତି
opts.D: ସାଇକେଲ ଚାଳକ ଦ୍ରୁତ ଗତିରେ ତଳକୁ ଓହ୍ଲାଇ ଯାଉଛନ୍ତି

miscon.text: A flat line on a distance-time graph was read as 'moving at a steady speed'. A flat line there means the cyclist has stopped.
miscon.skill: Preparing and explaining graphical data, for instance, plotting distance against time graphs.

miscon.subject: SST
miscon.stem: ପାହାଡିଆ ଅଞ୍ଚଳରେ ବାନାଗ୍ନି ସାଧାରଣ କାରଣ କେଉଁଟି ?

opts.A: ବିଜୁଳି ଦ୍ବାରା ଶୁଷ୍କ ଗଛରେ ନିଆଁ ଲାଗିବ।
opts.B: ପ୍ରାକୃତିକ ଢାଲୁ ନିଆଁ କୁ ବଢ଼ାଇବାରେ ସାହାଯ୍ୟ କରେ।
opts.C: ଲିଭି ନଥିବା ନିଆଁ କିମ୍ବା କ୍ୟାମ୍ପ ଅଗ୍ନି ରୁ ନିଆଁ ଲାଗିବ।

miscon.text: Forest fires were blamed on lightning. The common cause in hilly areas is an unextinguished campfire, that is, human carelessness.
miscon.skill: Explain the causes of forest fires, landslides, man-made disasters, and ways to mitigate disasters.

miscon.subject: Odia
miscon.stem: ଲେଖା ଆଧାରରେ, ସବୁଠାରୁ ଯୁକ୍ତିସଙ୍ଗତ ମତ କେଉଁଟି?

opts.A: ବିଶ୍ୱରେ ଜଙ୍ଗଲ ଆବରଣ ବଢ଼ିଛି, ତେଣୁ ଜଙ୍ଗଲ ସଂରକ୍ଷଣ ପାଇଁ ଚେଷ୍ଟା ନ କଲେ ଚଳିବ ।
opts.B: ଜଙ୍ଗଲ ଆବରଣ ବଢ଼ୁଛି, ତଥାପି ସୁରକ୍ଷିତ ଅଞ୍ଚଳ କମ୍ ଥିବା ଏକ ଗୁରୁତ୍ୱପୂର୍ଣ୍ଣ ଚିନ୍ତା।
opts.C: ପ୍ରତ୍ୟେକ ଦେଶ ସମାନ ଭାବରେ ଜଙ୍ଗଲ ସଂରକ୍ଷଣରେ ଅଂଶ ନ ନେବା ଚିନ୍ତାଜନକ ।
opts.D: ଜଳବାୟୁ ପରିବର୍ତ୍ତନ ଓ ଜଙ୍ଗଲ ଅଞ୍ଚଳ କମିବା- ଏମାନଙ୍କ ମଧ୍ୟରେ କିଛି ସମ୍ପର୍କ ନାହିଁ।

miscon.text: Children chose a general fairness statement over the inference the passage supports, that protected forest area stays too low even as forest cover rises.
miscon.skill: Read various types of materials in the Odia language (including newspapers, magazines, stories, and internet publications) to understand and write your preferences, comments, and expressions in the language or in braille
